scenario: IRGC seizes MSC Francesca + Epaminondas in Hormuz (Apr 22, CNBC, Al Jazeera) hours after ceasefire extension; Iran: 'extension has no meaning'; Brent $101.73 confirmed (+3.3% Apr 22); blockade remains; Iran holds 3 Western vessels; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; tail risk 40%

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Wheat rebounds on IRGC ship seizures (Apr 22) — tail-risk premium revives. P&I cancellation, blockade, and Cape routing cost premium remain intact. Fertilizer supply disruption mechanism unchanged. Source: Barchart ZWK26 (est.).

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Biofuel correlation rises with Brent ~$102.50 on IRGC ship seizures. Indonesian ethanol demand trajectory elevated; fertilizer and freight disruption mechanism unchanged — blockade intact; tail 40%.

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Bunker costs rise with Brent ~$102.50 on IRGC ship seizures. Indonesian and Vietnamese crush margins compress. Blockade intact — Cape routing costs elevated; structural supply chain disruption unresolved; tail 40%.

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: CPO rises with Brent ~$102.50 on IRGC ship seizures. Indonesia B40 mandate provides structural floor. Malaysia energy emergency (June supply uncertain, RON95 quota cut) adds domestic cost pressure. Fertilizer disruption for Q2 planting cycle unresolved. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD.

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: ASEAN food security pressure resurfaces on IRGC ship seizures. Q2 planting season urea availability risk persists — blockade intact, Qafco force majeure unresolved. Malaysia June supply gap risk adds to regional food security concern.

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption.

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Naphtha rises with Brent ~$102.50 on IRGC ship seizures. Blockade remains — Gulf condensate interdiction persists; Qatar force majeure unresolved; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026 (The National). Pengerang RAPID feedstock exposure elevated as Malaysia June supply risk grows. Source: ICIS/Platts (est.).

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Ethylene follows naphtha higher on IRGC ship seizures. SABIC and QAPCO export bottlenecks persist — blockade intact; vessel interdiction not lifted. Agricultural film and irrigation pipe lead times remain extended. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia (est.).

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Methanol edges higher on IRGC ship seizures. US Iran sanctions waiver expiry (Apr 19) tightens Iranian methanol enforcement. Qatar force majeure unresolved; blockade intact — freight disruption persists. China MTO complexes see incremental feedstock pressure. Source: Investing.com CFR China (est.).

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Asian planting windows create an acute near-term demand cliff.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Urea rebounds on IRGC ship seizures — remains severely elevated. Qafco force majeure persists; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; blockade intact — Gulf fertilizer export disruption unresolved. US sanctions waiver expiry (Apr 19) tightens Iranian-origin enforcement. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: DAP rebounds on IRGC ship seizures. Saudi Ma'aden shipping still disrupted; US Gulf supply stretched; blockade intact. Wet-season planting window in Philippines and Vietnam remains acute — IRGC seizures do not resolve supply chain.

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Potash freight costs edge up on IRGC ship seizures. Less directly exposed to Hormuz than urea or DAP — freight remains main transmission. Cape rerouting costs persist with blockade intact; palm oil plantations in Malaysia and Indonesia face moderately elevated input costs.

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Container lines have activated war risk surcharges and re-routed services, extending Asia-Europe transit times. Bunker fuel costs elevated by crude spike compound freight economics.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: BDI rises on IRGC ship seizures — Cape rerouting demand accelerates; blockade and P&I suspension persist. Normalization remains distant: no Hormuz reopening, no P&I restoration. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News (est.).

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: VLCC rates surge on IRGC ship seizures — blockade intact, P&I suspension unresolved, no Hormuz reopening. Iran holds 3 Western commercial vessels; tail (40%) would push rates to cycle highs. Source: Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List (est.).

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: Container rates rise on IRGC ship seizures. Cape rerouting premium intensifies — blockade intact, P&I unresolved. MSC Francesca seizure directly targets major container operator; Asia-Europe normalization requires Hormuz reopening + P&I restoration. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange (est.).
